examples: But dialects like Franglais, Japlish and Denglish are mixtures of English and local languages.
Despite rules requiring advertising slogans in English to be sub-titled, French manufacturers brazenly borrow English words to confect brands in franglais.
In France the young use an increasing number of English words, including main verbs, and say it is "cool" to accept the triumph of "franglais" (as their elders call it).
